description: Eval question set for Arkon chat. Edit freely — `id` is the only field referenced by runs/scores.

questions.title: CRISPR-Cas9 vs Cas12
questions.question: Compare CRISPR-Cas9 and CRISPR-Cas12. Cover: (a) mechanism of action, (b) PAM requirements, (c) typical applications, and (d) key practical differences (cut type, multiplexing, size).
expected_keypoints: Cas9 makes blunt double-strand cuts; Cas12 makes staggered cuts with 5' overhangs
Cas9 PAM = NGG; Cas12a PAM = TTTV (5' of protospacer)
Cas12 processes its own crRNA → easier multiplexing
Cas12 is smaller (~1200 aa) than Cas9 (~1400 aa) — easier delivery

questions.title: EU AI Act provisions taking effect in 2026
questions.question: What major provisions of the EU AI Act take effect in 2026, and what specifically do they require of providers of general-purpose AI models (GPAI)?
expected_keypoints: Most obligations apply 24 months after entry into force (Aug 2, 2026)
GPAI provider obligations: technical documentation, training data summaries, copyright policy
Systemic-risk GPAI (>10^25 FLOPs): incident reporting, adversarial testing, cybersecurity

questions.title: Multi-tenant pgvector at SQL layer
questions.question: How would you design a multi-tenant pgvector schema where each tenant's embeddings are isolated at the SQL layer — not by partitioning? Cover the relevant Postgres features (RLS, GUCs, role demotion) and the main tradeoffs.
expected_keypoints: RLS policies keyed on a tenant_id column
current_setting('app.tenant_id', true) inside policy, set via SET LOCAL per transaction
Connection-level role demotion (SET LOCAL ROLE) to NOSUPERUSER NOBYPASSRLS
Tradeoffs: index efficiency on large fan-out, query planner stats per-tenant, BYPASS RLS for admin paths

questions.title: Anthropic vs OpenAI prompt caching
questions.question: Compare Anthropic's prompt caching with OpenAI's prompt caching. Cover: cache scope (per-key, per-org), TTL behavior, pricing model, and the API ergonomics for marking cacheable spans. When does each provider win for which use case?
expected_keypoints: Anthropic: explicit cache_control breakpoints in messages; 5 min default TTL; per-org
OpenAI: automatic on >1024 tokens; 5–10 min TTL; opaque
Anthropic discounts cache reads ~10x; OpenAI ~50%
Anthropic wins for long-system-prompt agents; OpenAI for incidental savings

questions.title: Hybrid retrieval + reranker tradeoffs
questions.question: Explain hybrid retrieval combining BM25, dense vectors, and reciprocal rank fusion (RRF). At ~100k-document scale, when does adding a cross-encoder reranker help vs hurt? Cover latency, cost, and recall/precision tradeoffs concretely.
expected_keypoints: RRF: 1/(k+rank) sum across rankers; k=60 typical; rank-only, score-free fusion
Cross-encoder reranker: ~20–100ms / pair × ~100 candidates → 2–10s added latency
Help: noisy first-stage, long queries, fine-grained relevance
Hurt: already high precision, latency budget < 200ms, cost-sensitive paths

questions.title: LangGraph vs LlamaIndex Workflows vs hand-rolled loop
questions.question: Compare LangGraph, LlamaIndex Workflows, and a hand-rolled Claude tool-use loop. Cover code maintenance cost, debuggability, observability hooks, and which one you'd pick for: (a) a 2-engineer startup, (b) a 50-engineer enterprise rebuild, (c) a research prototype.
expected_keypoints: LangGraph: graph DSL + state, strong observability via LangSmith, dependency-heavy
LlamaIndex Workflows: event-driven, lighter, less mature
Hand-rolled: minimal deps, full control, must build retries/observability yourself
Startup → hand-rolled; enterprise → LangGraph; research → hand-rolled

questions.title: RLHF sycophancy and mitigations (DPO, CAI, KTO)
questions.question: Why does RLHF tend to produce sycophantic responses, and how do DPO, constitutional AI, and KTO try to mitigate this? Cite specific papers and the year each was introduced.
expected_keypoints: RLHF reward model rewards agreement → policy drift toward sycophancy
DPO (Rafailov 2023): direct preference optimization, skip reward model
Constitutional AI (Bai 2022): self-critique with principles before RLHF
KTO (Ethayarajh 2024): prospect-theoretic loss; binary feedback only

questions.title: MCP server vs Anthropic-built tool
questions.question: What's the difference between an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and an Anthropic-built tool, from a security and operations perspective? Cover: who runs each, where credentials live, audit trail differences, and which is more appropriate for accessing internal corporate data.
expected_keypoints: MCP server: customer-hosted (or self-hosted), credentials never leave customer infra
Anthropic-built tool: hosted by Anthropic, credentials passed through API
Audit: MCP audit is on customer side; Anthropic tool audit is in Anthropic logs
Internal data → MCP wins (data residency)

questions.title: Zero-downtime migration to RLS multi-tenancy
questions.question: Outline the steps to migrate a single-tenant SaaS to row-level-security multi-tenancy on PostgreSQL 15+ with zero downtime. What can go wrong at each step, and how would you roll back?
expected_keypoints: Step: add tenant_id (nullable) → backfill → NOT NULL → policies → demote role
Risks: index bloat during backfill; missing tenant_id breaks RLS silently; admin paths need BYPASSRLS
Rollback: drop policies (instant); keep tenant_id column for forensics

questions.title: Evaluating agentic LLM apps in production
questions.question: What's the current best practice for evaluating agentic LLM applications in production? Cover what to log, sampling strategy, and how to avoid LLM-as-judge drift over time.
expected_keypoints: Log: full traces (tool calls, inputs/outputs), latency per step, model version
Sample: stratified by user intent + outcome (success/error/escalation)
Anti-drift: pin judge model version, periodically re-score golden set, ensemble judges, human spot-checks
